method: read-only PostgREST (SUPABASE_URL + SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY), no direct Postgres connection used

membership_integrity.status: PASS -- every cluster has exactly one membership

duplicate_group_id_not_used.basis: computeBackfillPlan()'s BackfillPropertyListingRow type carries no duplicate_group_id field at all -- structurally impossible for this backfill to have read or written it. Verified again by re-inspection of the applied SQL: zero reference to duplicate_group_id.

no_invented_provenance.basis: identical to the frozen plan (plan_sha256 96289d98fc852d49cdaf9131aa22ce7822c3e6e7fb64def6a290102e499ba1b9), confirmed stable pre- and post-freeze

anonymous_access_denied.basis: structural -- the applied SQL contains zero CREATE POLICY statements (verified by re-inspecting apply.sql); RLS on all 4 tables was already enabled by the original Foundation migrations and is unchanged by this backfill. Live-tested with a real Supabase-equivalent anon/authenticated/service_role model in Gate B on byte-identical SQL logic.
anonymous_access_denied.status: PASS (via structural + Gate B equivalence, no live anon-key probe against Production -- no SUPABASE_ANON_KEY available in this environment)

idempotence_note: Not re-exercised against Production (would require a second real apply, out of scope/unnecessary risk). Idempotence (UPDATE guard clause + INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, 0-diff second apply) was rigorously proven in both Gate A (PGlite) and Gate B (real PostgreSQL 18.2) using byte-identical SQL-generation logic.
